debug_info.goal:
case inr
A B : Islander
hAB : Islander.Said A (Islander.isKnave A ∨ Islander.isKnave B)
hA : Islander.isKnave A
this : ¬(Islander.isKnave A ∨ Islander.isKnave B)
⊢ Islander.isKnight A ∧ Islander.isKnave B

failed_attempts:
have h₂ : A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave := Or.inl hA
    exact absurd h₂ h₁

have h₃ : A.isKnave := hA
    have h₄ : A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave := Or.inl h₃
    exfalso
    exact h₂ h₄

have h_or : A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave := Or.inl hA
    exact h h_or

exact h₂ h₁
    -- From h, we know that neither A nor B is a knave, which contradicts the fact that A is a knave.
    exfalso
    have h₁ : A.isKnave := hA
    have h₂ : A.isKnave or B.isKnave := Or.inl h₁
    exact h h₂

exact h₂
      -- Derive a contradiction from h and h₁
      exact h₁ h
    have h_main : A.isKnight and B.isKnave := by
      exfalso
      exact contradiction
    exact h_main

exact h₆
        exact h₅
      have h₃ : A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave := Or.inl h₁
      exact h₂ h₃
    have h_main : A.isKnight and B.isKnave := by
      exfalso
      exact h
    exact h_main

tauto
      tauto
      <;> simp_all [A.isKnave]
      <;> tauto
    have h₂ : A.isKnave := hA
    have h₃ : ¬(A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave) := h₁
    exfalso
    have h₄ : A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave := by
      exact Or.inl h₂
    exact h₃ h₄

have h₁ : A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave := Or.inl hA
    exfalso
    exact h h₁

have h₂ : A.isKnave := hA
    have h₃ : A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave := Or.inl h₂
    exfalso
    exact h₁ h₃

have h₁ : A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave := Or.inl hA
    exfalso
    exact h h₁

exact h₁ h'
    -- Since A is a knave, we have a contradiction because A is a knave but ¬(A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave) is false
    exfalso
    have h₁ : A.isKnave := hA
    have h₂ : A.isKnave or B.isKnave := Or.inl h₁
    exact h h₂

tauto
    have h₂ : ¬A.isKnave := by
      intro h₂
      have h₃ : A.isKnave or B.isKnave := Or.inl h₂
      contradiction
    have h₃ : A.isKnight := notisKnave_isKnight A h₂
    exfalso
    have h₄ : A.isKnave := hA
    have h₅ : ¬A.isKnight := isKnave_notisKnight A h₄
    exact h₅ h₃

tauto
    -- Since A is a knave, A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave is true (because A.isKnave is true)
    -- But h₁ says it's false, which is a contradiction
    exact (h₁ (Or.inl hA)).elim
successful_attempts:
simp [hA] at this
    <;>
    (try contradiction)
    <;>
    (try tauto)
    <;>
    (try
      {
        simp_all [isKnight_or_isKnave, not_isKnight_and_isKnave]
        <;>
        tauto
      })

simp at this ⊢
    <;> tauto
    <;> simp_all [Islander.isKnight_notisKnave]
    <;> tauto

exfalso
    apply this
    exact Or.inl hA

have h := this
    simp [hA] at h
    <;>
    (try tauto)
    <;>
    (try
      {
        simp [hA] at *
        <;>
        (try tauto)
      })
    <;>
    (try
      {
        simp_all [isKnight_notisKnaveIff]
        <;>
        tauto
      })
